perturbation.original_text: 1.9 The Sub-Advisor shall act honestly, with due skill, care and diligence and fairly and in the best interest of the Partnership in carrying out its obligations under this Agreement and shall use all reasonable endeavours to perform its obligations under this Agreement in accordance with FSMA, the FCA Rules and any other laws, regulations, guidelines and guidance as may be in force from time to time and applicable to the Funds and their business or to the Sub-Advisor ("Applicable Law").
perturbation.changed_text: 1.9 The Sub-Advisor shall act honestly, with due skill, care and diligence and fairly and in the best interest of the Partnership in carrying out its obligations under this Agreement, but is not required to use all reasonable endeavours to strictly perform its obligations under this Agreement. The Sub-Advisor will endeavor to act in accordance with FSMA, the FCA Rules and any other laws, regulations, guidelines and guidance as may be in force from time to time and applicable to the Funds and their business or to the Sub-Advisor ("Applicable Law").
perturbation.explanation: The change removes the explicit requirement for the Sub-Advisor to use 'all reasonable endeavours' to perform its obligations. This introduces ambiguity regarding the level of effort required, potentially contradicting Clause 7.1, which references a standard of care. The original text implies a strong obligation to perform, while the modified text suggests a more flexible approach. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty around the Sub-Advisor's required level of commitment and whether a failure to 'strictly perform' constitutes a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 In providing its services under this Agreement, the Sub-Advisor will discharge its duties in accordance with the same standard of care established for Oaktree US in the relevant Fund Agreements, and will be indemnified by each of the Funds as an agent of Oaktree US in accordance with such Fund Agreements. To the extent Oaktree US and its affiliates, directors, officers, employees, shareholders, assigns, representatives or agents (apart from the Sub-Advisor) (collectively, "Oaktree US Indemnities") suffer any liability, loss (including amounts paid in settlement), damages or expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) (collectively "Losses") in connection with the Funds, and:- (a) Oaktree US Indemnities are not indemnified by the Funds for such Losses under the indemnification provisions of the applicable Fund Agreements; (b) such Losses were suffered by virtue of the Sub-Advisor's or its employees' acts or omissions, or alleged acts or omissions under this Agreement; and (c) the Sub-Advisor (including its employees) is guilty of negligence or wilful misconduct, then the Sub-Advisor will hold Oaktree US Indemnities harmless and indemnify it for such Losses; provided that the Sub-Advisor shall not be liable for actions or omissions to act ordered by Oaktree US to which the Sub-Advisor objected in writing at the time of such order.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Clause 1.9 required the Sub-Advisor to use 'all reasonable endeavours' to perform its obligations, implying a strong duty of performance. The changed text removes this explicit requirement, stating that the Sub-Advisor is 'not required to use all reasonable endeavours to strictly perform its obligations'. This weakens the obligation and introduces ambiguity. Clause 7.1 requires the Sub-Advisor to discharge its duties in accordance with a specific standard of care, creating a direct contradiction because the changed text no longer guarantees a consistent or clearly defined level of effort in fulfilling those duties. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the perturbation explanation and definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, making enforcement of the standard of care in Clause 7.1 uncertain and contentious.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 In consideration of the provision of services under this Agreement, Oaktree US will pay the Sub-Advisor such fees as may be agreed between the parties from time to time (the "Service Fee").
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 In consideration of the provision of services under this Agreement, Oaktree US may, at its sole discretion, pay the Sub-Advisor such fees as may be agreed between the parties from time to time (the "Service Fee").
perturbation.explanation: The change replaces 'will pay' with 'may, at its sole discretion, pay,' introducing uncertainty as to whether Oaktree US is actually obligated to pay the agreed fees. This contradicts the implied obligation created by the phrase 'in consideration of the provision of services.' The original clause suggests a mandatory payment in return for services, while the modified clause makes the payment optional. This directly effects Clause 3.2 which calculates the Service Fee, but under this perturbation, the fee may not be mandatory anymore and at Oaktree US' discretion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 At Oaktree US' discretion, the Service Fee shall be reduced by any management fees received directly by the Sub-Advisor for investment management services provided to any party pursuant to this Agreement. The Service Fee shall also be reduced by any amounts earned on cash and cash-equivalents held by the Sub-Advisor pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modification changes 'will pay' to 'may, at its sole discretion, pay,' which introduces a direct contradiction with the implied obligation that Oaktree US is required to pay the Sub-Advisor. This directly contradicts the purpose of Clause 3.2, which calculates the 'Service Fee', as Oaktree US now has the discretion to not pay, rendering the calculation potentially irrelevant and creating uncertainty about whether the fee is mandatory or optional. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as described in the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2 Early Termination This Agreement may be terminated, either in respect of a Fund or in its entirety, by either Oaktree US or the Sub-Advisor for any reason upon 30 days' written notice to the other.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2 Early Termination This Agreement may be terminated, either in respect of a Fund or in its entirety, by either Oaktree US for any reason or the Sub-Advisor for a material breach of this agreement, upon 30 days' written notice to the other.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows either party to terminate the agreement 'for any reason.' The modified version restricts the Sub-Advisor's right to terminate to cases of 'material breach,' while Oaktree US retains the right to terminate for any reason. This change introduces an asymmetry in termination rights. This creates an in-text contradiction with clause 10.2, where both parties have the right to termination for any reason, and 11.1, where it speaks of a smooth transition but it is contradictory since Sub-Advisor may have a limitation on leaving the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 Upon the termination of this Agreement, the Sub-Advisor shall co-operate with Oaktree US and take all reasonable steps requested by Oaktree US in making an orderly transition to allow for continuity of management and to ensure that such termination shall not prejudice the completion of transactions already initiated.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an asymmetry in termination rights, limiting the Sub-Advisor's ability to terminate the agreement to cases of material breach, while Oaktree US retains the right to terminate for any reason. This directly contradicts the original intent reflected in clause 11.1, which speaks of a smooth transition upon termination, implying both parties have equal rights to terminate, causing uncertainty and potential for disputes, and creating an in-text contradiction.
